name: PlantVillage
description: PlantVillage is an open access library on crop health.

taxonomy.category: PlantVillage Updated on January 20, 2025 · Created on September 22, 2016 PlantVillage Open-source PlantVillage is an open access library on crop health. Developed By David Hughes Tested By Pennsylvania State University Content Partners Unknown Info Tags Open-source Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description PlantVillage is a user moderated Q&A forum dedicated to the goal of helping people grow their own food. It is an open resource that helps farmers solve plant-related questions. PlantVillage will offer the Nuru app , a digital assistant to help farmers diagnose crop disease in the field, without an internet connection. Work in progress includes drones, mobile spectrophotometry and testing use of satellites for smallholder farmers. Target SDGs SDG 2: Zero Hunger SDG 1: No Poverty Target Users (Target Impact Group) Household Distributors / Implementing Organizations PlantVillage is a non-profit project and a collaboration between Penn State University and EPFL. Regions Sub-Saharan Africa Countries India, United States Manufacturing/Building Method N/A Intellectural Property Type Open-source User Provision Model Users can access PlantVillage directly through their website. It is free to sign-up and become a member. Nuru app can be downloaded from the Google Play Store and Apple App Store. Distributions to Date Status PlantVillage has seen over 3 million unique users since launch in 2013.Interview with representative Design Specifications PlantVillage offers agricultural advice through multiple platforms on their website. Users can upload questions and pictures directly to the forum section of their website, where other users can then respond. Additionally, PlantVillage offers a plant database of nearly 200 plants with information on physical characteristics, maintenance needed, and harvesting tips. Lastly, users can create plant journals which help them track progress over the plant's life, as well as allowing them to identify when certain problems arose. Technical Support Support is provided directly by community members on the site.
